description: A stale CI cache serves old artifacts so a build that should fail passes, until a cache miss exposes the real breakage later.
body: Failure mode: a too-broad cache key reuses old dependencies or build artifacts, so a change that actually breaks the build keeps passing on cached content, then fails unexpectedly on a fresh runner or after cache eviction. Detection signals: builds pass locally with cache but fail on clean runners; failures appear after cache rotation unrelated to recent commits; lockfile changes not reflected in the cache key. Do not trust a green build that depends on a stale cache. Safer fallback: scope cache keys to the right invalidation inputs, periodically run cache-cleared builds, and confirm the build passes from a clean cache before trusting it.
